decks.notes: The deck focuses on assembling Lotus Field and using it along with cards like Scapeshift to generate a large amount of mana quickly. The main win condition is to ramp up mana using various cards such as Arboreal Grazer and Elvish Reclaimer to cast powerful spells like Terror of the Peaks or big creatures like The Wandering Minstrel. The deck also includes options for card draw and protection, making it a versatile combo deck capable of quickly overwhelming opponents with its mana generation and powerful threats.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo with cards like Parhelion II, Greasefang Okiba Boss, and Fleeting Spirit to create a powerful threat that is difficult for opponents to deal with. It also includes disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and protect the combo pieces. The deck runs a mix of lands to ensure consistency and flexibility in mana production. Monument to Endurance and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional support and synergy with the combo strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost and aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also includes spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage for removal and damage. The inclusion of Stormchaser's Talent and Experimental Synthesizer allows for additional card draw and potential for extra damage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a mix of creature aggression and direct damage spells, while also utilizing land cards efficiently to maintain a steady flow of resources.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain a fast and aggressive playstyle utilizing a combination of quick and efficient spells, along with creatures that can deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of low-cost spells and creatures, while also utilizing cards that can generate additional card advantage to keep the pressure on. With a mix of burn spells, cheap creatures, and card draw effects, the deck aims to quickly close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent early with efficient creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, backed up by disruptive cards like Thalia, Heretic Cathar and Archon of Emeria. The deck also includes removal options like Skyclave Apparition and Enduring Innocence to deal with opposing threats. The mana base is optimized to support the aggressive game plan with a mix of dual lands and mana ramp. Collected Company provides card advantage and the ability to quickly refill the board with creatures.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, creature removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, and powerful threats such as Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Ouroboroid. The main goal is to control the board and outvalue the opponent by efficiently dealing with threats while developing your own board presence. The deck also utilizes land destruction in the form of Culling Ritual and Unholy Annex to further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly establish a board presence and apply pressure to the opponent. Utilizing low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with efficient removal spells like Reckless Rage and Burst Lightning, the deck aims to deal damage early and often. Additional spells like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent provide card advantage and ways to pump up your creatures for extra damage. The mana base is optimized for consistency and allows for quick access to the deck's various spells and threats.

This UW Control deck focuses on using a variety of control spells and removal to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage through cards like Consult the Star Charts and Memory Deluge. The deck also includes a mix of planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to help maintain board control. The deck aims to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent with its card advantage and removal spells, ultimately leading to a victory through attrition.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on utilizing cheap spells and creatures to deal damage quickly and efficiently. By using cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger alongside burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent early in the game. The versatility of cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics allow for disruption against opposing strategies, while cards like Heroes' Hangout and Stormchaser's Talent provide card advantage to maintain momentum. Overall, the deck is designed to be fast-paced and aggressive in order to overwhelm opponents.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly deploy low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, and then pump them up with spells like Monstrous Rage and Stormchaser's Talent to deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. The deck also utilizes disruptive spells like Spell Pierce and Fiery Impulse to control the board and maintain pressure. Academic Dispute, Opt, and Consider help with card selection and ensuring efficient use of resources. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive game plan with a mix of lands that provide both red and blue mana.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of versatile removal spells, flexible counter-magic and powerful threats. Niv-Mizzet Reborn serves as a card advantage engine and win condition, providing access to a wide range of cards. The deck also includes a companion, Yorion, Sky Nomad, to further enhance its control elements. The mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the diverse spells in the deck, allowing for consistent casting of powerful multicolored cards. Ultimately, the goal is to outvalue the opponent and secure victory through superior card advantage and board control.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate large amounts of mana quickly, enabling powerful combo plays with cards like Scapeshift and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods. The deck also includes cards like Arboreal Grazer and Crumbling Vestige to help ramp up mana production even further. The strategy revolves around setting up and protecting Lotus Field, while efficiently executing game-winning combos to overwhelm the opponent. Murmurers of this strategy suggest that it can be quite potent in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches due to its ability to quickly assemble powerful board states.

The strategy of this aggro Red Deck Wins Pioneer deck revolves around quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost, high-impact creatures and burn spells. By efficiently managing resources and maximizing damage output each turn, this deck aims to close out games as quickly as possible before the opponent can stabilize. Cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage pair well with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike to chip away at the opponent's life total, while cards like Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Monstrous Rage provide additional reach and damage potential.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal, and card draw to maintain board control and outvalue the opponent. It also utilizes Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion for additional value and synergy with the deck's creature cards. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually building up its own resources and eventually winning through card advantage and powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. With a mix of control elements and win conditions, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents in a slow and calculated manner.

The deck focuses on using a mix of removal spells and disruptive creatures to control the game, while also generating card advantage by blinking permanents with Yorion, Sky Nomad. By efficiently removing threats and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a strong board presence and close out the game with powerful finishers like Beza, the Bounding Spring and Rite of Oblivion. The deck also utilizes a variety of lands to ensure consistency and mana fixing throughout the game.

This deck focuses on a control strategy using efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes mana dorks like Llanowar Elves to ramp up into powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by generating card advantage with cards like Liliana of the Veil and Boseiju, Who Endures, while also being able to deal with a variety of threats using cards like Abrupt Decay and Go for the Throat.
